title: System Response to External Input: The Zero-State Response
summary: This page presents Example 3.22, using MATLAB's filter command to compute and plot the zero-state response of a discrete-time system, noting that the output is unbounded because the system is unstable. It then introduces the response of LTID systems to complex inputs and states that real and imaginary input components generate corresponding real and imaginary output components, followed by a brief discussion of multiple-input superposition.
